top_comments.text:
Agree...

Look at “Managing the Development of Large Software Systems” (Royce 1970) Figure 10 on scanned page 338:

https://www.praxisframework.org/files/royce1970.pdf

Whatever you do, do not stop on Figure 2's infamous waterfall!

Understand Royce understood Figure 4, and in Figure 7 proposed prototyping with code to inform the product (and iterate the product).

T
Creator here. Yes I did. Used some existing puzzles as inspiration. See https://praxispuzzles.com/calendar_puzzle_square

Recently I designed a calendar puzzle with 10 tetris-like pieces. When you place all the puzzle pieces on the board, three squares/rhombuses are still open and together they form a date. Can you arrange the puzzle pieces in such a way that it shows todays date? See https://praxispuzzles.com/calendar_puzzle_rhombus Disclaimer: I sell these puzzles for a little more than the raw materi

> So we're doing waterfall again?

The waterfall we know was always a mistake. The downhill only flow we know and (don't) love was from someone at DOD who only glanced at the second diagram (Figure 2) in the original 1970 Royce paper and said "This makes sense, we'll do it!" and... we're doing waterfall.

So, go to the paper that started it all, but was arguing against it:

-

Royce's original "Waterfall" paper[1] is significantly more iterative & agile than Scrum! It's outdated since at the time (1970) programming was done mostly on paper & then run later on a mainframe or special-purpose computer, but many of the core ideas still hold true for modern programming environments.

Of course many of the organizations that claimed to be implementing Waterfall omitted the iterative steps, then wondered why it didn't work. That also happens

contrarian_analysis.bull_case_flaw: PraxisPro touts impressive ROI metrics and AI-powered simulations, but offers no concrete, third-party validated evidence or case studies to back up these claims—just generic testimonials and vague promises.
contrarian_analysis.elephant_in_room: How does PraxisPro actually outperform existing LXPs or sales training platforms already used by life sciences giants, and what unique data or outcomes can they prove beyond marketing fluff?

timing_assessment.reasoning: AI-driven training is hot, but the market is crowded and pharma is risk-averse; unless PraxisPro shows clear, differentiated impact, they may be lost in the noise or outpaced by bigger, faster incumbents.

incumbent_threat.most_dangerous_competitor: Microsoft (Viva Learning + Azure OpenAI), Google (Cloud AI), OpenAI (custom enterprise GPTs)
incumbent_threat.killer_feature: A major player launches integrated, compliance-ready AI roleplay and certification modules for pharma—bundled with existing enterprise tools and data security guarantees.
incumbent_threat.time_to_threat: 3-6 months; incumbents can build and deploy similar features quickly, leveraging existing pharma relationships and infrastructure.

hidden_assumptions: Pharma companies will trust a small vendor with sensitive compliance and training data.
PraxisPro’s SLMs are meaningfully better than off-the-shelf LLMs for pharma use cases.
Sales teams will actually use and engage with simulated AI roleplays at scale.
Regulatory approval for AI-driven training content will be straightforward and fast.

moat_reality.claimed_moat: Purpose-built SLMs, pharma compliance, tailored simulations, measurable ROI.
moat_reality.actual_moat: Minimal; SLMs are not defensible if incumbents build verticalized models, compliance is table stakes, and simulations are easily replicable with LLM APIs.

contrarian_analysis.honest_take: PraxisPro is a slick pitch with pharma buzzwords, but without hard proof or technical differentiation, they’re vulnerable to being steamrolled by enterprise incumbents. Unless they show real, measurable impact—fast—they risk being just another AI startup lost in the hype cycle.

primary_trigger.trigger: Recent advances in generative AI, specifically large language models tailored for life sciences, have enabled context-aware, regulatory-compliant training experiences.
primary_trigger.evidence: Rapid adoption of specialized AI models in pharma sales enablement platforms; funding rounds for AI-powered tools in 2024.
primary_trigger.timing: Late 2023 to early 2024, with mainstream deployment in Q1 2024.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Pharmaceutical and medical device companies have dramatically increased digital training budgets post-pandemic.
secondary_triggers.relevance: PraxisPro can capitalize on surging demand for scalable, effective sales training solutions.

secondary_triggers.trigger: FDA and EMA have tightened compliance requirements for sales training documentation and audit trails.
secondary_triggers.relevance: AI-powered platforms like PraxisPro can automate compliance tracking, giving them an edge over manual or legacy systems.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Incumbents like Allego and Showpad have lagged in adopting vertical-specific AI, focusing on generic features.
secondary_triggers.relevance: PraxisPro’s life-sciences-first approach fills a gap left by slower-moving competitors.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Hybrid and remote work models have become the norm for pharma sales teams, increasing the need for asynchronous, personalized training.
secondary_triggers.relevance: PraxisPro’s AI adapts to individual learning styles, meeting modern workforce expectations.

why_now.why_not_earlier: Two years ago, AI models lacked the domain-specific accuracy and regulatory awareness needed for life sciences training, and pharma companies were less open to digital transformation.
why_now.why_not_later: Delaying risks losing first-mover advantage as competitors race to integrate advanced AI; pharma clients are actively seeking solutions now due to compliance and remote work pressures.
why_now.newsletter_hook: AI breakthroughs and new compliance rules mean pharma sales teams need smarter, faster training—now.

takeaways.title: Deploy Custom Small Language Models per Client
takeaways.insight: PraxisPro's use of custom SLMs for each client and therapeutic area enables hyper-personalized, compliant AI simulations. This approach improves relevance, privacy, and regulatory alignment versus generic LLMs.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Identify key client verticals or domains, fine-tune small LLMs (e.g., using LoRA or QLoRA) on proprietary datasets for each, and deploy them in isolated environments with domain-specific guardrails.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your clients have low data volume or lack domain-specific requirements, or if compliance is not a major concern, generic LLMs may suffice.
takeaways.example:
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('praxispro/clientA-therapeutic-slm')
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('praxispro/clientA-therapeutic-slm')

takeaways.title: Leverage SLMs for Lower Inference Costs
takeaways.insight: Small, domain-specific models consume fewer resources during inference, reducing cloud GPU/CPU costs compared to large, general-purpose LLMs.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Benchmark model sizes and throughput for your use case, select open-source SLM architectures (e.g., Phi-2, TinyLlama), and deploy using quantization or serverless endpoints.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your application needs broad generalization or multi-domain knowledge, SLMs may underperform and require fallback to larger models.
takeaways.example:
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B')
# Use bitsandbytes for quantized inference
model = model.quantize(4)

takeaways.title: Real-Time Roleplay via Persona Meshes
takeaways.insight: PraxisPro enables real-time, configurable persona simulations by preloading SLMs with persona-specific prompt templates, minimizing latency and maximizing scenario flexibility.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Design persona prompt templates and cache them with SLM endpoints. Use lightweight routing logic to select persona at runtime and pre-warm endpoints for instant response.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your product doesn't require real-time interaction or persona switching, this optimization adds unnecessary complexity.
takeaways.example:
persona_prompt = persona_templates['skeptical_specialist']
input = persona_prompt + user_input
output = slm.generate(input)

takeaways.title: Immediate Feedback Loops in Simulation APIs
takeaways.insight: PraxisPro delivers instant feedback to users during simulations, increasing engagement and learning speed. This is enabled by tight integration between simulation logic and analytics.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Expose feedback endpoints in your API, connect simulation events to analytics pipelines, and return actionable feedback with each interaction.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your platform is asynchronous or batch-based, immediate feedback may not be feasible or relevant.

takeaways.title: Vertical Data Moats for Competitive Advantage
takeaways.insight: PraxisPro builds proprietary datasets for each therapeutic area, creating defensible data moats and enabling better model fine-tuning and compliance.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Segment data collection and annotation by vertical, establish secure pipelines for ingesting client-specific data, and use this data to continuously fine-tune SLMs.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your business is horizontal (cross-industry) or lacks access to proprietary data, vertical moats may be impractical.

quick_wins: Add immediate feedback endpoints to your simulation APIs
Benchmark SLMs (e.g., TinyLlama, Phi-2) for cost and latency savings
Design persona prompt templates for your main user scenarios
deeper_dives: Fine-tuning SLMs with LoRA/QLoRA for hyper-personalization
Building compliance-aware guardrails for healthcare AI
Architecting data pipelines for vertical-specific model training

headlines.headline: Why PraxisPro is betting AGAINST giant LLMs for pharma sales AI
headlines.subheadline: Instead of chasing large models, PraxisPro is doubling down on micro-model meshes—can small really win in regulated life sciences?

headlines.headline: I reverse-engineered PraxisPro’s SLM mesh—here’s what makes their pharma AI tick
headlines.subheadline: Dissecting PraxisPro’s custom language models reveals a surprising focus on vertical data moats and compliance over raw scale.

headlines.headline: PraxisPro is running the Snowflake playbook—vertical data moats, but for life sciences AI
headlines.subheadline: By building domain-specific SLMs, PraxisPro is hoping to lock in pharma clients the way Snowflake did with data warehousing.

headlines.headline: The micro-model mesh every AI team should steal from PraxisPro
headlines.subheadline: PraxisPro’s approach to SLMs could be the blueprint for faster, more compliant AI in regulated industries.

headlines.headline: Is PraxisPro’s SLM strategy just pharma hype—or a real moat?
headlines.subheadline: With $6M in fresh funding, PraxisPro’s bet on small, vertical models faces a tough test: can they outpace incumbents before the hype fades?

viral_hooks.best_headline: Why PraxisPro is betting AGAINST giant LLMs for pharma sales AI. This headline directly challenges industry orthodoxy, highlights PraxisPro’s unique technical choice, and creates curiosity about whether their contrarian bet will pay off—making it highly relevant and scroll-stopping for an expert audience.
viral_hooks.social_media_version: Why PraxisPro is betting AGAINST giant LLMs for pharma sales AI

hook.opening_line: What if pharma sales reps could train with AI that knows every objection, every product nuance, and never gets tired?
hook.stakes: Billions in lost sales and sluggish product launches hinge on how quickly life sciences teams ramp up and adapt.
hook.why_now: With generative AI reshaping every knowledge job, pharma sales training is the next high-stakes frontier.

discovery.surface_story: PraxisPro promises smarter, faster training for pharmaceutical and medical device sales teams using AI-powered simulations.
discovery.deeper_story: Behind the pitch, PraxisPro is quietly ditching generic LLMs in favor of custom-tuned Small Language Models and building granular, persona-driven HCP simulations—delivering not just content, but real-time, scenario-based feedback.
discovery.surprise: Unlike most platforms, PraxisPro claims direct, measurable impact on sales outcomes—tying AI-driven learning activities to ROI metrics that matter to the C-suite.

analysis.technical_insight: PraxisPro’s micro-model mesh and continuous-learning flywheel enable domain-specific, low-latency simulations, while their analytics architecture links training to business results.
analysis.competitive_insight: Their technical edge is real but fragile—incumbents could catch up fast if PraxisPro can’t prove out-of-the-box impact.
analysis.tension: Bull case: PraxisPro sets a new benchmark for AI in pharma sales, carving a defensible niche. Bear case: Without rapid, visible wins, they get drowned out by bigger players and AI hype fatigue.

resolution.takeaway: For builders, the future of AI training is real-time feedback, domain-specific models, and measurable business value.
resolution.prediction: Expect a wave of copycats and consolidation—PraxisPro’s survival hinges on proving ROI before the giants move in.
resolution.call_to_action: If you’re building in AI or edtech, study PraxisPro’s approach to feedback loops and SLMs—or risk being left behind.

suggested_visuals: Diagram: Micro-model mesh vs. generic LLM architecture
Chart: Claimed ROI metrics (sales lift, ramp time reduction, confidence boost)

unique_voice_content:
```markdown
## PraxisPro: Pharma Sales Training Meets Custom AI, But Is It More Than Just Flash?

Ever wished the AI in your sales training actually knew the difference between a monoclonal antibody and a statin? PraxisPro bets you do—and they've built a platform of tailored small language models that claim to understand both. The twist? They're promising not just smarter simulations, but real business impact and compliance you *might* actually trust.

### Core Insight

Most AI training platforms are like generic gym memberships: lots of shiny equipment, but nobody showing you how to use them for your goals. PraxisPro, in contrast, is trying to be your clinical-grade personal trainer, designing custom Small Language Models (SLMs) for every disease state, therapeutic area, and sales scenario. If they pull it off, it means pharma reps get more relevant practice, faster feedback, and (in theory) less regulatory headache. But the real question is—are their SLMs the equivalent of a bespoke coach, or just a slightly fancier treadmill?

### Technical Meat

PraxisPro’s architecture veers off the well-trodden LLM superhighway and into the land of micro-model meshes—think lots of small, specialized brains, each laser-focused on a niche. This is a bold move. SLMs are theoretically faster, more private, and easier to wrangle for compliance than a behemoth like GPT-4. In pharma, where latency, privacy, and audit trails are non-negotiable, that’s a clear technical win.

But then there’s the compliance circus. PraxisPro claims their platform only delivers content that’s been through the regulatory wringer (PRC/PRB/MLR), with real-time audit tracking. Anyone who’s tried to automate pharma workflows knows this isn’t trivial; most incumbents punt this to human reviewers or clunky legacy systems. If PraxisPro has actually solved real-time regulatory enforcement, color me impressed—but right now, we only have their word for it.

The red flag? Their ROI metrics are all sizzle, no steak: “40% increase in sales outcomes, 50% ramp time reduction!”…but no detailed case studies, no third-party validation, not even a technical blog post. Just generic testimonials and pharma buzzwords. On the engineering side, their stack is a black box (no open source, no public jobs, no technical details) and HackerNews sentiment is, predictably, skeptical. In short: interesting architecture choices, but zero transparency.

### Honest Assessment

Would I use PraxisPro? If I ran a pharma sales org desperate for compliance and tailored training, I’d at least take the demo. But as a builder, I’m not buying until they show *actual* technical differentiation and hard outcomes. Right now, it’s a slick pitch vulnerable to being flattened by enterprise incumbents who can build verticalized models in a few months. If you’re allergic to hype and want proof, wait and watch.

### Builder Takeaway

- **SLMs aren’t magic:** Niche models can be smarter and faster, but incumbents can copy this playbook overnight. Don’t bet your moat on size alone.
- **Compliance is table stakes:** Real-time regulatory enforcement? Cool, if real. But in regulated verticals, everyone needs it, and it’s only a differentiator if your workflows are provably better.
- **Show, don’t tell on ROI:** If your platform claims business impact, publish the data—case studies, third-party audits, technical deep-dives. Builders and buyers alike see through empty numbers.
- **Transparency matters:** If you want technical talent and trust, open up your stack, share your engineering challenges, and engage skeptics. Silence breeds suspicion.
- **Personalization is a feature, not a moat:** If your defense is “hyper-customized for every client,” ask if legacy players can outspend you on the same idea.

### Prediction

PraxisPro is either acquired by a legacy pharma tech vendor craving AI cred, or buried under the next wave of “vertical LLM” startups. If they publish real, third-party validated impact in the next year—and open up their tech story—they might actually stick around.
```
